Race screen — 2026-06-24 · updated 11:16 · auto-refreshing

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn) / pace✓ (run-style suits the course's pace bias). The yellow pace line also says if a track is front-runner-favouring or a hold-up track (from drawbias.com).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 0/5 (0%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 0/5 (0%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-22 · updated 09:03

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn) / pace✓ (run-style suits the course's pace bias). The yellow pace line also says if a track is front-runner-favouring or a hold-up track (from drawbias.com).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 13/14 (93%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 10/14 (71%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-21 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn) / pace✓ (run-style suits the course's pace bias). The yellow pace line also says if a track is front-runner-favouring or a hold-up track (from drawbias.com).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 12/13 (92%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 8/13 (62%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-20 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn) / pace✓ (run-style suits the course's pace bias). The yellow pace line also says if a track is front-runner-favouring or a hold-up track (from drawbias.com).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 14/23 (61%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 9/23 (39%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-19 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn) / pace✓ (run-style suits the course's pace bias). The yellow pace line also says if a track is front-runner-favouring or a hold-up track (from drawbias.com).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 19/29 (66%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 15/29 (52%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-18 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn) / pace✓ (run-style suits the course's pace bias). The yellow pace line also says if a track is front-runner-favouring or a hold-up track (from drawbias.com).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 12/18 (67%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 8/18 (44%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-17 · updated 09:00

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn) / pace✓ (run-style suits the course's pace bias). The yellow pace line also says if a track is front-runner-favouring or a hold-up track (from drawbias.com).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 13/20 (65%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 10/20 (50%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-16 · updated 09:00

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (handles today's track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn for this course, trip & going).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green, ✓ = a gold pick won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in your top 3 today: 6 of 17 races (35%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-15 · updated 09:48

Each race is ranked by your data — the top 3 rows are your top 3 selections, with a faint line under them. Front-runners in orange, prominent in yellow, hold-up in grey; green course record = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running. The Res column fills with finishing positions when you re-run after racing, the winner turns green, and a ✓ on a race means one of your top 3 won. Speed is a rough home figure — a flag, not a tip.

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-14 · updated 09:47

Each race is ranked by your data — the top 3 rows are your top 3 selections, with a faint line under them. Front-runners in orange, prominent in yellow, hold-up in grey; green course record = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running. The Res column fills with finishing positions when you re-run after racing, the winner turns green, and a ✓ on a race means one of your top 3 won. Speed is a rough home figure — a flag, not a tip.

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-13 · updated 11:45

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: ★ EYE finished last run well   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (your 1–4)   ▼ easy trk hard→easy drop-in (a plus)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (handles today's track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn for this course, trip & going).
Rest: front-runners orange, prominent yellow, hold-up grey; green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed races = novice or 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally). Res fills in after racing (winner green, ✓ = a gold pick won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in your top 3 today: 23 of 35 races (66%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-12 · updated 09:39

Each race is ranked by your data — the top 3 rows are your top 3 selections, with a faint line under them. Front-runners in orange, prominent in yellow, hold-up in grey; green course record = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running. The Res column fills with finishing positions when you re-run after racing, the winner turns green, and a ✓ on a race means one of your top 3 won. Speed is a rough home figure — a flag, not a tip.
